OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
I think the best way to handle conversations on COVID vaccines is to be honest but not too pushy. I'm an electrician in Naples, so I deal with blue-collar work most days. Most of my interactions are practical ones—wiring, tools, fixing stuff—but sometimes people bring up sensitive topics like this. Being anxious doesn't help much, you see; it makes me want to avoid big discussions if possible. Still, when someone asks, I try to answer from what I know and respect my family and God.

I remember reading about how the vaccines work in some public health materials during the pandemic, even though my education is only upper secondary, but that diploma helped me understand basic things. My dad didn't have much schooling; he was more into doing practical stuff all day long—like repairing our home or teaching me about mechanics—but I learned from him too.

So when someone asks me about COVID vaccines on social media sometimes, which is a few times weekly but not daily anyway because my hobbies like cycling and reading novels are quieter things mostly—I just explain it step by step without getting into arguments.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I'm an electrician with moderate anxiety. My education level suggests I can handle factual info if presented clearly. Being anxious makes me cautious in discussions, so I'd prefer straightforward answers rather than complex debates. Respecting Islamic beliefs means acknowledging the importance of community and family opinions on such topics. Social media use is infrequent but could expose me to divisive views.
citations: Various sources including public health guidelines
